meta.description: HTTPokémon — HTTP status codes illustrated with scenes from the Pokémon anime and games.
hero.sub: HTTP Status Codes illustrated with scenes from the Pokémon anime and games.
status.unknownDesc: This code is not in the HTTP Pokédex.
status.imgMissingBody: Add the file images/{code}.jpg to the project root for it to appear here.
footer.developedBy: Developed by alce
footer.inspiredBy: Inspired by the classic format of HTTP Cats
footer.disclaimer1: Legal Notice / Disclaimer: This is a fan project, strictly personal, educational and non-commercial in nature. Images, names and concepts related to Pokémon are registered trademarks and intellectual property of Nintendo, Creatures Inc., Game Freak and The Pokémon Company.
footer.disclaimer2: No copyright infringement is intended. All original content on this site is distributed free of charge and generates no direct or indirect revenue.

100.desc: Professor Oak says: keep going! Part of the request was received — you may proceed.

101.desc: Evolution in progress! The server is switching protocols as requested by the client.

102.desc: Pokémon is being trained… The server received the request and is processing it, but no response yet.

103.desc: Pre-loading activated! The server sends early hints to help the client prepare resources.

200.desc: Pokémon caught! The request succeeded and the response is here.

201.desc: New Pokémon registered in the Pokédex! Resource created successfully.

202.desc: Mission accepted by the Bonfire Clan! The request was accepted but processing isn't done yet.

203.desc: Third-party info — like Team Rocket's Codex. Data comes from an intermediary source.

204.desc: Pokémon fled… without a trace. Success, but no response body.

205.desc: Pikachu hit reset! The client should clear the current form or view.

206.desc: Pokémon mid-battle! Only part of the resource was delivered (range request).

207.desc: Full team report! Multiple statuses in a single XML response.

208.desc: Already reported to the Professor! This binding was already included in a previous response.

226.desc: Pokédex delta applied! The server applied an IM transformation to the response.

300.desc: Which starter will you choose? The resource has multiple representations available.

301.desc: Gym Leader moved to another city — permanently! The resource has moved for good.

302.desc: Pokémon spotted around here (temporarily)! Temporary redirect.

303.desc: Look over there! The result is at another URI — use GET to fetch it.

304.desc: Nothing changed since the last battle! Cache is still valid, no need to retransmit.

307.desc: Temporary route on the Pokémon map! Temporary redirect preserving the HTTP method.

308.desc: New official route on the map! Permanent redirect preserving the HTTP method.

400.desc: Invalid command! The server couldn't understand the request due to malformed syntax.

401.desc: You need a Badge to enter! Authentication required but not provided.

402.desc: You need PokéCoins! Reserved for future use, but commonly used for paywalls.

403.desc: Team Rocket restricted area! Authenticated but no permission to access.

404.desc: Pokémon not found! The requested resource doesn't exist on this server.

405.desc: That move isn't allowed here! The HTTP method used isn't supported for this resource.

406.desc: Pokémon won't accept that Poké Ball! The server can't generate a response in the requested format.

407.desc: Pokémon Center guard asks for ID! Proxy authentication required.

408.desc: Time ran out to choose a move! The client took too long to send the request.

409.desc: Two Pokémon, one Trainer — conflict! The current resource state conflicts with the request.

410.desc: Pokémon was released back into the wild! The resource is gone permanently.

411.desc: How large is your request? Content-Length is required for this request.

412.desc: Battle precondition not met! A header condition evaluated to false.

413.desc: Snorlax is too heavy! The request payload exceeds the server's limit.

414.desc: Path to the Gym is too long! The provided URI is larger than the server accepts.

415.desc: Server doesn't accept that Poké Ball format! The request media type is not supported.

416.desc: Invalid XP range! The requested byte range is outside the file's bounds.

417.desc: Battle expectation failed! The server couldn't satisfy the Expect header.

418.desc: I'm a Magikarp, not a Gyarados! RFC 2324 — the server refuses to brew coffee because it's a teapot.

421.desc: Pokémon sent to the wrong Gym! The request was directed to an inappropriate server.

422.desc: Malformed Pokémon in the Poké Ball! The server understood the type but can't process the instructions.

423.desc: Pokémon using Iron Defense! The resource is locked.

424.desc: The battle chain broke! The request failed because a previous one did.

425.desc: Too early to evolve! The server won't risk processing a request that might be replayed.

426.desc: Needs to evolve! The client must switch to a different protocol.

428.desc: Battle condition required! The server requires the request to be conditional.

429.desc: Too many Poké Balls at once! The client sent too many requests in too short a time.

431.desc: Ash's backpack is overflowing! The request headers are too large.

444.desc: Pokémon vanished without responding! The server closed the connection without sending any response.

451.desc: Banned by the Pokémon League! The resource is unavailable for legal reasons.

499.desc: The Trainer abandoned the battle! The client closed the connection before the server responded.

500.desc: The server got confused like a Psyduck with a headache! Unexpected internal error.

501.desc: Move not yet learned! The server doesn't support the required functionality.

502.desc: Mt. Moon gateway blocked! The server received an invalid response from an upstream server.

503.desc: Pokémon Center closed for maintenance! The server is temporarily unavailable.

504.desc: Timed out in the Rocket corridor! The upstream server took too long to respond.

505.desc: Incompatible Pokédex version! The server doesn't support the HTTP version used in the request.

507.desc: Bill's PC is full! There's not enough space to complete the operation.

508.desc: Pokémon spinning in circles! The server detected an infinite loop while processing the request.

511.desc: Authentication required on the Gym's network! Network authentication is required to gain access.

599.desc: Network signal as weak as Magikarp! Timed out while trying to connect to the network.
